response:
Sec. 1  
peanuts are a type of food that contains proteins which can trigger allergic reactions in some people  
allergic reactions can cause symptoms such as difficulty breathing and swelling of the lips  
these reactions can be severe and require immediate medical attention  
people who are allergic to peanuts cannot eat them and must avoid them in all forms  
the question is about why they can't live underwater  
the reason is related to the physical properties of peanuts and their inability to withstand underwater conditions  

Sec. 2  
peanuts are not designed to survive in water and their composition does not allow them to function in aquatic environments  
the structure of peanuts is not adapted for submersion and they will decompose or become unusable in water  
this makes it impossible for people who are allergic to peanuts to live underwater  
the allergic reaction is unrelated to the physical properties of peanuts and their ability to exist in water
